title: End of module: Further Mechanics: Circular Motion and SHM

questions.text: A car travels at $v = 20\,\text{m}\,\text{s}^{-1}$ around a circular bend of radius $r = 50\,\text{m}$. Calculate the centripetal acceleration.
The car in the previous question has mass $1200\,\text{kg}$. Calculate the centripetal force needed to keep it on the bend.
In which direction do the centripetal acceleration and centripetal force always point, for an object moving in a circle?
choices: Away from the centre of the circle
Tangentially, in the direction of motion
Towards the centre of the circle
Vertically downwards, due to gravity, regardless of the circle's orientation
questions.explanation: Centripetal ('centre-seeking') acceleration and force always point towards the centre of the circular path — this is what continuously turns the velocity vector without changing its magnitude.

questions.text: A wheel completes one full revolution every $8\,\text{s}$. Calculate its angular velocity $\omega$ (to 3 s.f.).
A point on a spinning disc of radius $0.4\,\text{m}$ moves with angular velocity $5\,\text{rad}\,\text{s}^{-1}$. Calculate its linear (tangential) speed.
Which equation correctly defines simple harmonic motion?
choices: $a = \omega^2 x$
$a = -\omega^2 x$
$a = -\omega x^2$
$a = \omega x$
questions.explanation: SHM requires acceleration proportional to displacement but directed back towards equilibrium: $a = -\omega^2 x$. The minus sign is essential — it makes the motion oscillate rather than accelerate away from equilibrium.

questions.text: An oscillator has amplitude $A = 0.05\,\text{m}$ and angular frequency $\omega = 4\,\text{rad}\,\text{s}^{-1}$. Calculate its maximum speed.
For the same oscillator ($A = 0.05\,\text{m}$, $\omega = 4\,\text{rad}\,\text{s}^{-1}$), calculate its maximum acceleration.
questions.explanation: Maximum acceleration occurs at $x = \pm A$: $a_{\max} = \omega^2 A = 4^2 \times 0.05 = 16 \times 0.05 = 0.8\,\text{m}\,\text{s}^{-2}$.

questions.text: For the same oscillator ($A = 0.05\,\text{m}$, $\omega = 4\,\text{rad}\,\text{s}^{-1}$), find its speed when its displacement is $x = 0.03\,\text{m}$.
A mass $m = 0.2\,\text{kg}$ oscillates with amplitude $A = 0.05\,\text{m}$ and angular frequency $\omega = 4\,\text{rad}\,\text{s}^{-1}$. Calculate its total mechanical energy.
Calculate the period of a simple pendulum of length $l = 0.5\,\text{m}$. Use $g = 9.81\,\text{m}\,\text{s}^{-2}$.
A mass $m = 0.4\,\text{kg}$ is attached to a spring of spring constant $k = 25\,\text{N}\,\text{m}^{-1}$. Calculate the period of oscillation.
A door closer is designed to return a door to its closed position as quickly as possible, without the door swinging back and forth. Which type of damping does it use?
choices: Light damping
Critical damping
Heavy (over) damping
No damping (free oscillation)
questions.explanation: Critical damping returns a system to equilibrium in the shortest possible time without any oscillation — exactly what is wanted for a door closer or a car's suspension.

questions.text: Resonance occurs in a forced oscillation when…
choices: the driving frequency is much less than the natural frequency
the driving frequency equals the natural frequency of the system
the driving frequency is much greater than the natural frequency
the system is critically damped
questions.explanation: Resonance — a large-amplitude response — occurs when the driving frequency matches the system's own natural frequency of free oscillation.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about energy in an undamped (free) simple harmonic oscillator are correct?
choices: The total mechanical energy remains constant throughout the motion
The kinetic energy is at its maximum at maximum displacement
The potential energy is at its maximum at maximum displacement
The kinetic energy is at its maximum when the oscillator passes through the equilibrium position
questions.explanation: With no resistive forces, total energy is conserved. At maximum displacement the oscillator is momentarily at rest, so KE is zero and all the energy is potential (making the second statement false and the third true). At equilibrium the speed — and hence KE — is greatest.

questions.text: What term describes the frequency at which a system oscillates freely, with no external driving force and no damping?
questions.explanation: This is called the **natural frequency** of the system. Resonance occurs when an external driving frequency matches it.
